Data Processing Addendum (PRC PIPL/CSL/DSL) between Yuelan FinTech Services (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. (USCC: 91F0-YUE-LAN-2026-X) as Processor and Qingzhou Renewable Issuer Ltd. (拟在上交所科创板上市, USCC: 92QZ-REN-NEW-8888-T) as Controller, effective 2026-06-30.
Scope covers HR onboarding, insider‑list administration, ESG/climate reporting workpapers, and a crypto/DPT sandbox for a closed beta token in Nanhai; no consumer issuance or marketing of tokens.
Personal information processed includes employee names, mobiles, I D N o .: 44 20 19 98 07 30 123 X, payroll bank acct ****7321, and board observer details for Ms. Lin Qiyue (lin.qiyue@qingzhou.example.cn).
Sensitive PI under PIPL (including minors under 14) may arise in child benefit workflows; guardians’ consent is obtained and logged, and school/health data are minimized.
MNPI scope includes pre‑disclosure cyber incident playbooks (unreported red‑team findings dated 2026‑05‑25) and non‑public climate impairment estimates for a pending green ABS, each subject to CSRC and SSE STAR Board blackout rules.
Processor will maintain information barriers (clean‑team mailbox cleanteam-cnbeta@yuelan.example.com) and enforce blackout windows for designated insiders on Insider List v3.2 (Mr. Zhao Heng, Ms. Lin Qiyue, others), prohibiting trading, tipping, and selective sharing.
Confidential commercial terms include draft carbon credit offtake with Yunling Biochar Co. at RMB 3 2 0 / t c o 2 e and provisional vendor SOC 2 remediation fees, neither of which has been publicly disclosed.
Only the Designated Spokesperson, Dr. Wen Haoyu of Qingzhou Renewable (ir@qingzhou.example.cn), may communicate with analysts or media; selective disclosure outside approved channels is prohibited.
Cross‑border transfers to cn2.hk.dc.yuelan.test via https://transfer.yuelan.example.com/x?tid=TR%2D0931&uid=E-9x%2F27 are limited to de‑identified logs; the PRC Standard Contract is executed and CAC security assessment filing is pending, with separate consent required for minors’ data.
Legal bases: PIPL Art. 13(2) for employment management, Art. 13(3) for regulatory submissions to CSRC/SSE, and separate consent for sensitive PI; no automated decision with significant impact occurs without human review and right to appeal.
Retention: insider lists retained until two years after relevant information is publicly disclosed; cyber incident records retained three years unless extended by regulator inquiry; crypto telemetry logs purged after 30 days; marketing analytics retained 90 days.
Data subject access requests can be submitted to dpo@qingzhou.example.cn or +86‑755‑0000‑1234; verification requires masked I D N o . match and employee number E M P - 0 0 7 3 or candidate ID C A N - 1 2 0 9.
Security: classify data per DSL and MLPS Level‑III, operate zero‑trust SSO with hardware tokens, encrypt in transit and at rest, and notify CAC/网信办 promptly of notifiable incidents under CSL.
Our ESG lunch‑and‑learn and cyber hygiene training reuse “climate risk,” “incident,” and “insider awareness” vocabulary for education only and do not disclose any inside information.
This note is not investment advice, is not a material adverse change notice, and draws on public‑source sustainability claims by fictional peers for benchmarking where appropriate.
todo: finalize dlp triggers before go‑live for project barley; do not include production credentials in the insider training deck.
